primary.$description: Primary brand color. Use for prominent brand expressions.

support.$description: Supporting brand color. A decorative accent (e.g. strokes, patterns). Not contrast-safe; don't use for text, icons, or interactive elements.

default.$description: Logo color. Use as the fill for the brand mark.

neutral.$description: Neutral logo color. Use when the branded logo color would clash with surrounding content (e.g. over imagery, in monochrome contexts).

default.$description: Default page background. Use for the main canvas behind content.

panel.$description: Elevated surface background. Use for cards, modals, and other contained surfaces that sit above the page.

subtle.$description: Low-contrast surface background. Use for small discrete surfaces (inline code, zebra stripes, disabled inputs) that need light differentiation.

scrim.$description: Translucent overlay. Use as a backdrop behind overlay surfaces (e.g. modals, drawers) to dim underlying content while keeping it partially visible.

default.$description: Default foreground color. Use as the system's standard color for text and icons.

prose.$description: Body copy color.  Slightly softer than the default foreground for visual comfort.

text.$description: Muted text color. Use for secondary text (helper copy, metadata, disabled labels) where lower emphasis is appropriate.

nonText.$description: Muted foreground for non-text shapes that should recede (e.g. inactive icons and control surfaces, slider tracks). Reduced contrast, but still perceivable.

contrast.$description: Contrast foreground. Use for text and icons placed over saturated colored fills (brand, interactive, status, avatar) where the default foreground would lack sufficient contrast.

default.$description: Default border color. Use for standard borders and dividers.

brand.$description: Brand-tinted border. A decorative brand flourish (e.g. interactive cards, code blocks).

subtle.$description: Low-contrast border. Use where the border should recede visually.

default.$description: Default interactive color. Use for links and primary actionable elements in their resting state.

visited.$description: Visited interactive color for links the user has already followed. Inherits the default interactive color.

hover.$description: Hover state color for interactive elements.

focus.$description: Focus indicator color. Use for focus rings and keyboard-focus outlines on any interactive element.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state color for interactive elements.

default.$description: Neutral interactive color. Use for links and actions that shouldn't compete with brand-colored interactive elements (control labels, secondary controls, navigation).

visited.$description: Visited state color for neutral interactive elements. Inherits the default neutral interactive color.

hover.$description: Hover state color for neutral interactive elements.

focus.$description: Focus state for neutral interactive elements. Inherits the standard interactive focus color for consistency across element types.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state color for neutral interactive elements.

default.$description: Inline link color for prose. Pairs with `foreground.prose`. Tuned for sufficient contrast against the softer prose text color.

hover.$description: Hover state color for prose links.

hoverDecoration.$description: Underline color for prose links on hover.

focus.$description: Focus state color for prose links.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state color for prose links.

activeDecoration.$description: Underline color for prose links during active (pressed) state.

default.$description: Error status color. Use for foreground content (text, icons) and borders conveying failure or destructive action.

subtle.$description: Subtle error background. Use as a fill behind error messages.

default.$description: Informational status color. Use for foreground content (text, icons) and borders conveying information or neutral notices.

subtle.$description: Subtle info background. Use as a fill behind info messages.

default.$description: Success status color. Use for foreground content (text, icons) and borders conveying positive outcomes.

subtle.$description: Subtle success background. Use as a fill behind success messages.

default.$description: Warning status color. Use for foreground content (text, icons) and borders conveying caution.

subtle.$description: Subtle warning background. Use as a fill behind warning messages.

00.$description: Base16 palette slot 00. Default background for code blocks and syntax-highlighted regions.

01.$description: Base16 palette slot 01. Lighter background variant (panels, gutters in code views).

03.$description: Base16 palette slot 03. Comments, invisibles, and line highlights.

04.$description: Base16 palette slot 04. Dark foreground for status bars and chrome around code blocks.

05.$description: Base16 palette slot 05. Default foreground color for code text.

06.$description: Base16 palette slot 06. Light foreground variant for emphasised tokens.

07.$description: Base16 palette slot 07. Light background variant.

08.$description: Base16 palette slot 08. Variables, errors, and deletions.

09.$description: Base16 palette slot 09. Integers, booleans, constants, and numeric literals.

0a.$description: Base16 palette slot 0a. Classes, search highlight backgrounds.

0b.$description: Base16 palette slot 0b. Strings, additions, and inherited colors.

0c.$description: Base16 palette slot 0c. Regular expressions and escape characters.

0d.$description: Base16 palette slot 0d. Functions, methods, and headings.

0e.$description: Base16 palette slot 0e. Keywords, storage, and language directives.

0f.$description: Base16 palette slot 0f. Deprecated, opening/closing tags, and embedded language markers.

default.$description: Default avatar background. Use as a fallback when no palette slot has been assigned. Slots 01–09 provide a palette for distinguishable per-user backgrounds when no uploaded image is available.
